title: IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scoring
summary: Replace static equal-weight signal scoring with a data-driven IC-weighted composite. Each of the 9 signal dimensions is weighted proportional to its recent Spearman IC (rank correlation against 5-day forward returns), computed on a rolling 60-trade window. Dimensions with no predictive power receive zero weight; the composite falls back to equal weighting until data is sufficient. Three progressive phases: Phase 1 (shipped — basic IC weights, no noise floor), Phase 2 (IC noise floor + Alphalens validation), Phase 3 (walk-forward optimization with HMM regime conditioning).

definition_of_ic.rationale: Rank-based rather than Pearson — robust to non-normal return distributions and outlier scores, which are common in short-window equity signals
definition_of_ic.x_variable: Z-scored dimension score at time of signal (zero mean / unit variance across the rolling window)
definition_of_ic.forward_price_source: yfinance 1d OHLCV — first available close >= 7 calendar days after scan date, which covers exactly 5 trading days in a standard week
definition_of_ic.staleness_guard: Records < 6 calendar days old excluded — forward return not yet available
definition_of_ic.library: scipy.stats.spearmanr (primary); numpy argsort approximation (fallback if scipy unavailable)

ic_thresholds.gt_0_05: Useful signal — weight assigned proportional to IC
ic_thresholds.0_03_to_0_05: Marginal — included in Phase 1 (any positive IC passes); suppressed in Phase 2+ by noise floor
ic_thresholds.lt_0_03: Noise floor — zero weight in Phase 2+
ic_thresholds.negative: Anti-predictive — zero weight in all phases (not inverted)

phases.label: Basic IC Weighting
phases.ic_noise_floor_note: No minimum threshold. Any positive IC receives proportional weight. Negative IC is clamped to zero.
phases.fallback_rule: If all IC <= 0 or fewer than 20 valid records: equal weights (1/9 per dimension)
phases.update_cadence: Weekly — bot.py Sunday review cycle (weekday() == 6) calls ic_calculator.update_ic_weights()
files: ic_calculator.py — core IC computation, weight normalization, cache I/O (418 lines)
signals.py — compute_confluence() applies IC weights before MTF penalty
bot.py — GET /api/ic_weights endpoint; Sunday update trigger
dashboard.py — IC weights panel with per-dim bars, raw IC, 4-week trend arrows
deliverables_done: Rolling Spearman IC per dimension (60-trade window, min 20 valid)
Z-score normalization of dimension scores before IC calculation
Dashboard IC weights panel: per-dim bars, raw IC, 4-week trend arrows, equal-weight fallback indicator
Backward-compatible: equal weights produce identical score to prior equal-weight sum on 0-50 scale
known_gaps: No IC noise floor — any positive IC (even 0.001) receives weight, including noise

phases.label: IC Noise Floor + Alphalens Validation
phases.min_trades_rationale: 200+ closed trades required for statistically meaningful IC estimates across at least 2 market regimes. At 5-10 paper trades per day, approximately 20-40 trading days of data.
phases.ic_noise_floor_rationale: IC < 0.03 is indistinguishable from noise per Grinold & Kahn (Active Portfolio Management, 2000). Suppressing noise-level dimensions reduces overfitting in the weight vector.
phases.weight_rule: weight_i = IC_i if IC_i >= 0.03 else 0; normalize so sum(weights) = 1.0
phases.fallback_rule: If all dimensions below noise floor or < 20 valid records: equal weights (1/9 per dimension)
phases.hhi_cap: If any dimension weight > 0.40 after normalization: clip to 0.40, renormalize remaining. Log WARNING.
deliverables: Add IC_MIN_THRESHOLD = 0.03 constant to ic_calculator.py (or config.py['ic_calculator']['ic_min_threshold'] — see open questions)
Modify normalize_ic_weights() to apply noise floor: weight_i = IC_i if IC_i >= IC_MIN_THRESHOLD else 0
Add HHI concentration cap: clip any weight > 0.40, renormalize, log warning
Add 'noise_floor_applied' flag and 'dimensions_suppressed' list to ic_weights.json cache record
Run alphalens-reloaded analysis: quantile returns, IC decay at T+1/T+5/T+20, turnover per dimension
Produce validation report: KEEP / REDUCE WEIGHT / REMOVE recommendation per dimension
Dimension-flag integration: auto-suggest dimension_flags[dim] = false for persistent IC < 0.03
Update test_ic_calculator.py to cover noise floor suppression and HHI cap cases

phases.label: Walk-Forward Optimization with HMM Regime Conditioning
phases.min_trades_rationale: 6+ months of regime-labeled trade data needed for walk-forward. ~200 trades per regime bucket (bull/bear/choppy) for stable per-regime IC estimates.
phases.weight_rule: final_weights = P(bull)*IC_weights_bull + P(bear)*IC_weights_bear + P(choppy)*IC_weights_choppy; apply noise floor per regime; renormalize

walk_forward.overfitting_guard: If out-of-sample IC degradation > 50% of in-sample: widen training window or constrain weights to [0.5x, 2.0x] around equal weight

phases.regime_router_interaction: Phase 3 per-regime IC weights subsume the VIX-based regime router (feat-regime-router). Disable regime_routing_enabled once Phase 3 HMM weights are stable for 4+ consecutive weekly snapshots with out-of-sample IC retention > 50%. Running both creates double-counting.
deliverables: Per-regime IC calculation: separate Spearman IC windows for bull / bear / choppy regime labels
HMM regime probabilities from BACK-003 (hmmlearn) as blending coefficients
Blended weight vector: sum over regimes of P(regime_r) * IC_weights_r, renormalized
Walk-forward validation: 6-month training window, 1-month test window, roll monthly
Overfitting guard: if out-of-sample IC degradation > 50%, widen window or constrain weights to [0.5x, 2.0x] around equal
Weight stability monitor: flag if any weight changes by > 50% week-over-week
Extend ic_weights.json schema with per_regime_ic and regime_probs fields
Extend ic_weights_history.jsonl with walk-forward validation metrics per snapshot

required_fields_per_record: price (scan-time price for forward return calculation — NOT entry price)
score_breakdown (dict with all 9 dimension scores keyed by dimension name)
primary_input.minimum_for_phase_1: 20 records with valid forward returns (equal weights fallback below this)
primary_input.minimum_for_phase_3: 600 closed trades across at least 2 market regimes

forward_return_source.method: _fetch_forward_returns_batch() in ic_calculator.py — batched by symbol to minimize API calls
forward_return_source.horizon: 5 trading days (first available close >= 7 calendar days after scan date)
forward_return_source.staleness_guard: Records < 6 calendar days old excluded — forward return not yet available
forward_return_source.fallback: None — records without forward returns are excluded from IC calculation (not imputed)

cache_files.ic_weights_json: data/ic_weights.json — current weight vector, raw IC per dimension, n_records, using_equal_weights flag, updated timestamp
cache_files.ic_weights_history_jsonl: data/ic_weights_history.jsonl — append-only weekly snapshots of same schema

phase_3_additional.regime_labels: HMM regime probabilities per signal record (added by BACK-003)
phase_3_additional.historical_data: 5yr OHLCV for SPY/QQQ for HMM training via yfinance

data_requirements.survivorship_bias_note: signals_log.jsonl captures all scored candidates above the conviction threshold, not only entered trades. This provides conditional-on-threshold IC (not unconditional). To reduce selection bias further, Phase 2 should evaluate whether logging below-threshold candidates is feasible.

signal_object.field_read_by_ic: dimension_scores (dict) — same as score_breakdown logged to signals_log.jsonl

composite_scoring_formula.formula: score = sum(weight_i * N_dims * dim_score_i for each dim) + candle_bonus
composite_scoring_formula.scaling_rationale: Factor of N_dims (9) ensures backward-compatibility: with equal weights (1/9 each), sum = sum(dim_scores), identical to pre-IC baseline on 0-50 scale
composite_scoring_formula.candle_bonus_note: Candlestick pattern bonus (0-3 points) is not a dimension — added on top of IC-weighted sum, not subject to IC weighting
composite_scoring_formula.mtf_penalty_note: MTF soft penalty applied after IC sum — order unchanged from Phase 1

weight_fetch.fallback: equal weights dict if file missing, malformed, or weights do not sum to ~1.0
weight_fetch.latency: File read only — no network call on hot path

integration_points.dimension_flags_interaction: Disabled dimensions (dimension_flags[dim] = false in config.py) receive score = 0 before IC weighting. In Phase 2, ic_weights.json 'dimensions_suppressed' field should union disabled_by_flag and suppressed_by_ic_floor for clear diagnostics.

primary.metric: Win rate of IC-weighted scoring vs equal-weight baseline
primary.target: >=5 percentage point improvement in win rate over a rolling 60-trade window
primary.measurement: Log ic_weights snapshot key with each trade in signals_log.jsonl. Compare win rates for trades executed during equal-weight fallback periods vs IC-weighted periods.

secondary.target: Rolling standard deviation of each dimension IC < 0.10 over 4 consecutive weekly snapshots
secondary.rationale: Unstable IC indicates noisy or regime-dependent predictive power that a single window cannot capture reliably

secondary.target: Walk-forward out-of-sample IC degradation < 50% of in-sample IC per dimension
secondary.rationale: Degradation > 50% signals overfitting; widen training window or constrain weights per roadmap/06

secondary.target: No single dimension exceeds 40% of total weight
secondary.rationale: Extreme weight concentration amplifies single-factor risk and is typically a transient IC spike artifact

secondary.target: using_equal_weights = false in >= 80% of weekly snapshots once 60+ trades accumulated
secondary.rationale: Persistent fallback indicates data pipeline issue — signals_log.jsonl not accumulating sufficient records with resolved forward returns

backtesting_comparison.baseline: Equal weights (1/9 per dimension) — the pre-36f9166 scoring
backtesting_comparison.comparison_2: IC-weighted with 0.03 noise floor + HHI cap (Phase 2)
backtesting_comparison.expected_ordering: Phase 3 >= Phase 2 > Phase 1 > equal weights on out-of-sample Sharpe ratio
backtesting_comparison.min_sample_for_comparison: 200 trades per comparison for statistical significance

open_questions.question: Should the IC noise floor (0.03) be a configurable constant in config.py or hard-coded in ic_calculator.py?
open_questions.recommendation: Add to config.py['ic_calculator']['ic_min_threshold']. Allows operational tuning without a code deploy, and signals it is a policy decision rather than a mathematical constant.

open_questions.question: Should the VIX-based regime router (feat-regime-router) be disabled when Phase 3 HMM weights go live?
open_questions.recommendation: Yes. Both systems modulate dimension weights by regime — running both double-counts the regime signal. Disable regime_routing_enabled after Phase 3 weights are stable for 4+ consecutive weekly snapshots.

open_questions.question: Should the HHI concentration cap (40% max weight) be enforced in normalize_ic_weights() or only monitored?
open_questions.recommendation: Enforce in Phase 2: clip any weight > 0.40, renormalize, log WARNING. Monitoring alone risks letting a transient IC spike silently distort the composite.

risks.mitigation: Phase 1 requires min_valid=20. Phase 2 requires 200+ trades. Phase 3 requires 600+ with regularization constraints (weight bounds [0.5x, 2.0x] around equal weight).

signals_log.jsonl captures all candidates above the scoring threshold, not only entered trades. Log scan-time price (not entry price) for forward return. Phase 2 to evaluate logging below-threshold candidates.

risks.risk: yfinance rate limits during batch forward return fetching
risks.mitigation: _fetch_forward_returns_batch() groups by symbol. update_ic_weights() called once per week (not hot path). Records without forward returns excluded, not imputed.

risks.risk: IC non-stationarity — weights from Q1 2026 may not generalise to Q3 2026
risks.mitigation: Rolling 60-trade window naturally downweights stale observations. Phase 3 monthly walk-forward retraining provides explicit adaptation. Weight stability monitor flags > 50% week-over-week changes.

risks.risk: Phase 3 HMM regime mislabelling corrupts per-regime IC training
risks.mitigation: Validate BACK-003 (HMM) independently before Phase 3. Phase 3 is explicitly blocked on BACK-003 completion and validation.

references: Grinold & Kahn, Active Portfolio Management (2000) — IC methodology and noise floor thresholds
Di Mascio, Lines, Naik, Alpha Decay (SSRN) — signal decay research
Stefan Jansen, Machine Learning for Algorithmic Trading (2020) — Chapter 4, factor validation
Dynamic Factor Allocation Leveraging Regime-Switching Signals (arXiv 2410.14841, 2024) — ML-based factor weight allocation; shows IR improvement from 0.05 (equal-weight) to ~0.40 (dynamic)
Pardo, The Evaluation and Optimization of Trading Strategies (2008) — walk-forward optimization methodology

BACK-005.original_title: Signal validation via Alphalens IC analysis
BACK-005.rationale: Phase 1 has already shipped the core IC computation. Alphalens full analysis (quantile returns, IC decay, turnover) is now Phase 2 of this spec. Keeping BACK-005 as a standalone item creates the false impression that no IC work has been done and obscures the phase sequencing.

BACK-006.rationale: roadmap/06 explicitly recommended starting with IC-proportional weights before walk-forward optimization. Phase 1 IS that simpler alternative. Walk-forward + HMM conditioning is Phase 3 of this spec. All Phase 3 deliverables and dependencies from BACK-006 are preserved here.

BACK-011.title: IC-Weighted Dynamic Scoring — Phase 2 (noise floor + Alphalens)
BACK-011.rationale: Superseding BACK-005 leaves Phase C empty. BACK-011 fills that gap and maintains Phase C as the active validation phase.
